([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Championnats du Monde de Ski de Montagne 2002*) was the first [World Championship of Ski Mountaineering](/wiki/World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "World Championship of Ski Mountaineering") sanctioned by the [International Council for Ski Mountaineering Competitions](/wiki/International_Council_for_Ski_Mountaineering_Competitions "International Council for Ski Mountaineering Competitions") (ISMC), held at [Serre Chevalier](/wiki/Serre_Chevalier "Serre Chevalier") in the former French province [Dauphiné](/wiki/Dauphin%C3%A9 "Dauphiné") from January 24 to January 27, 2002. In due to the heavy snowfall all events were postponed one day[[1]](#cite_note-Verschiebung-1)

first_paragraph: The **2002 World Championship of Ski Mountaineering** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Championnats du Monde de Ski de Montagne 2002*) was the first [World Championship of Ski Mountaineering](/wiki/World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "World Championship of Ski Mountaineering") sanctioned by the [International Council for Ski Mountaineering Competitions](/wiki/International_Council_for_Ski_Mountaineering_Competitions "International Council for Ski Mountaineering Competitions") (ISMC), held at [Serre Chevalier](/wiki/Serre_Chevalier "Serre Chevalier") in the former French province [Dauphiné](/wiki/Dauphin%C3%A9 "Dauphiné") from January 24 to January 27, 2002. In due to the heavy snowfall all events were postponed one day[[1]](#cite_note-Verschiebung-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **2002 World Championship of Ski Mountaineering** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Championnats du Monde de Ski de Montagne 2002*) was the first [World Championship of Ski Mountaineering](/wiki/World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "World Championship of Ski Mountaineering") sanctioned by the [International Council for Ski Mountaineering Competitions](/wiki/International_Council_for_Ski_Mountaineering_Competitions "International Council for Ski Mountaineering Competitions") (ISMC), held at [Serre Chevalier](/wiki/Serre_Chevalier "Serre Chevalier") in the former French province [Dauphiné](/wiki/Dauphin%C3%A9 "Dauphiné") from January 24 to January 27, 2002. In due to the heavy snowfall all events were postponed one day[[1]](#cite_note-Verschiebung-1)

The event was supported by the [Fédération française de la montagne et de l’escalade](/wiki/F%C3%A9d%C3%A9ration_fran%C3%A7aise_de_la_montagne_et_de_l%E2%80%99escalade "Fédération française de la montagne et de l’escalade") (FFME). The 230 participants were from 22 nations and from three continents. Compared to the 1975 [Trofeo Mezzalama](/wiki/Trofeo_Mezzalama "Trofeo Mezzalama"), which was also held as "World Championship of Ski Mountaineering" with the classes "Civilians", "Soldiers" and "Mountain guides",[[2]](#cite_note-Rolf_Majcen-2) the ICSM competition was only for members of the civilian national squads. Participants from countries without a squad could register with the help of the national mountain organizations.

## Equipment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2002_World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Equipment")]

The equipment of the participants was very different:

> The start line scene set the stage, with all the techno-light skis of the Europeans
>
> contrasted with a pair of ‘fat’ skis and then a pair of ‘tele’ skis representing Team
> Canada. Our race suits were [Gore-Tex](/wiki/Gore-Tex "Gore-Tex"), not [Lycra](/wiki/Lycra "Lycra"), and we had no logos. By the
> graciousness of some other countries being slower than us, we did not place last. The
> best line we heard was from the Polish team, whom we passed on the downhill at high
> speed saying, “You guys can sure ski down hill fast.” Which meant that we were very
>
> slow on the uphills![[3]](#cite_note-Haywood-3)

— team race's report of the Canadian athlete Richard Haywood, [ACMG](/wiki/Association_of_Canadian_Mountain_Guides "Association of Canadian Mountain Guides")

The weight of the ultra-light skis of the European top teams was only about [*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] including binding. These teams also had the mentioned race suits as well as long and light [Nordic skiing](/wiki/Nordic_skiing "Nordic skiing") [poles](/wiki/Ski_pole "Ski pole").
